title: Further Details About Expeditions
description: During this stay he sent 'Ubaydah b. al-Harith b. al-Muttalib at the head of eighty or sixty horsemen from the Emigrants without a single Ansari among them. He got as far as Ahya', a watering place in Hijaz, below the pass of al-Marah.26 There he met a great band of Quraysh, but there was no fighting between them, except that Sa'd b. Abi Waggas shot an arrow on that day, which was the first arrow shot in Islam. Then the two groups separated from one another, the Muslims leaving a rearguard. Al-Migdad b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 07
